items.title: Regional families and carers fear they'll bear the brunt of NDIS cuts

children.content: People living with disability in regional areas already face extra hurdles in accessing support and families fear changes to the NDIS could have irreversible consequences.

items.title: Three children attacked by dingoes in national park

children.content: Three children have been attacked by dingoes in Karijini National Park since Saturday, prompting a warning from authorities.

image.altText: A dingo howling towards the camera

items.title: Alleged threat to ‘blow up’ Perth Airport terminal sparks chaos, lands man in court

children.content: A man who allegedly threatened to "blow up" Perth Airport's main international terminal, forcing its evacuation and causing traveller chaos, has appeared in court.

image.altText: Keiron Beau James Hedges smiles.

items.title: From a local rink in Perth, figure skating teen makes history for Australia

children.content: At just 15, Hana Bath became the first Australian female to land a triple axel in competition. The rising star who began her journey on a rink in Cockburn now has her eye on the 2030 Winter Olympics.

Hana Bath holds the Australian flag while wearing her silver medal

items.title: Fight over legal costs from Gina Rinehart judgement begins

children.content: A legal wrangle is underway over what costs mining heiress Gina Rinehart is liable for following last week's judgement in her epic court battle with the family of her father's former business partner.

image.altText: Gina Rinehart smiles while standing in front of cattle pens

items.title: Husband of former WA politician faces court on 27 child sex abuse charges

children.content: Former Nationals and Independent MLC Louise Kingston watched on as her husband Garry faced fresh child sexual abuse charges in a WA court.

image.altText: A man and a woman cover their faces with pieces of paper as they walk past a brick building.

items.title: 'We're not ready to leave': Inside the messy dynamic of a no-grounds eviction

children.content: Evicting a tenant without cause offers landlords a simple way out, but the issue can be much more complex than it appears. One such example of that complexity is Eleanor Kahlo's case of her no-grounds eviction.

gallery.altText: A man with a vest on and a clipboard watches as Eleanor walks through a hallway.
Eleanor peers through blinds in a dimly lit, empty room.
Eleanor in the drivers seat of a van, looking out her side window.

items.title: 'Safer' digital drivers' licences to be rolled out in WA

children.content: The WA government allocates $28 million for the roll-out of digital drivers' licences in the state, estimated to be implemented by the end of next year.

image.altText: A woman holds a phone with a driver licence on the screen

children.content: Stolen cars and accommodation break-ins have led to growing frustration in Western Australia's north.

items.title: Farmers say an easy solution to the mouse plague has been rejected

children.content: Farmers are pushing for stronger bait approval as mice numbers soar out of control in Western Australia, but stronger poisons
are being rejected by the national chemical regulator.

items.title: Gas giants get resource for free? That's 'tosh', says King

children.content: Resources Minister Madeleine King
has taken aim at those who say gas giants are obtaining the commodity for free, saying she's "mystified as to how they get away with such tosh". Follow live.

image.altText: Madeleine King

items.title: Removing plastic from our food chain has quick results, WA researchers say

children.content: Perth researchers find levels of plastic chemicals in the body can be reduced within a week by avoiding plastic food packaging and kitchen utensils.

image.altText: Bottles, tin, glass

items.title: How Aussies near you are coping with the fuel crisis

children.content: Petrol prices are reshaping Australian life in ways big and small — from a grandmother's isolation to a farmer's future.

articles.title: Viva Energy boss says more refineries needed for secure fuel supply

children.content: The Viva Energy refinery provides about 10 per cent of Australia's fuel. Its chief, Scott Wyatt, chats to Alan Kohler about the company's future in the wake of the Geelong fire.

items.title: Oil prices spike again just as Australian fuel prices drop

children.content: The price of unleaded petrol is back to just 10 cents a litre more than before the war, even as oil prices are spiking again on global markets. Follow the day's events and insights as they happen with our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: 'Genuinely speechless': Appeal board's stunning claim in homophobia case

children.content: The appeal board in the Lance Collard homophobia case says homophobic and racist language is commonplace in the AFL due to the "highly competitive" nature of the game.

image.altText: Lance Collard stands with both arms in the air during a St Kilda vs West Coast Eagles AFL match in 2025.

items.title: Which states get a public holiday for Anzac Day?

children.content: Anzac Day is observed on April 25 every year. This year that date falls on a Saturday, but that doesn't mean a long weekend for everyone.

items.title: Now James Valentine is gone, I'll let you in on the secret of his success

children.content: Children grew up with him, adults grew old with him, he kept us connected to our city, the world and each other. James helped us make sense of life and gave us permission to laugh at the absurdity of it all — and ourselves.

image.altText: James Valentine, wearing a read jumper and black headphones, smiling and talking into a microphone.

items.title: 'Public health issue': Box jellyfish swarm spotted off WA coast
